metadata.description: Proximal tibial articular surface bearing medial and lateral condylar load.
Hindfoot bone articulating with talus at the subtalar joint.
Tarsometatarsal ligamentous and osseous disruption threatening midfoot stability.
Open reduction internal fixation of unstable Lisfranc injury.
Lateral pelvic ilium region relevant to acetabular column and pelvic ring injuries.
Plantar fascia and soft-tissue envelope relevant to calcaneus fracture swelling and wound risk.
Tarsometatarsal articulation complex; instability defines Lisfranc injury.
Posterior pelvic anchor articulating with ilia at the sacroiliac joints.
Anterior midline pelvic articulation; disruption indicates pelvic ring instability.
Posterior pelvic articulation between sacrum and ilium; key pelvic ring stability structure.

relationship_metadata.note: weight-bearing films and clinical exam confirm instability

metadata.claim_text: Lisfranc injury threatens tarsometatarsal stability and midfoot arch integrity — missed diagnosis leads to chronic collapse.
Document mechanism, plantar ecchymosis, weight-bearing alignment, stability exam, and operative versus immobilization plan.

metadata.action: Proceed with Lisfranc ORIF to restore midfoot column alignment; document postoperative protected weight-bearing
metadata.trigger: Unstable Lisfranc injury with tarsometatarsal diastasis or clinical instability on exam

metadata.action: Apply Lisfranc nonoperative immobilization with protected weight-bearing and close follow-up for secondary displacement
metadata.trigger: Nondisplaced stable Lisfranc injury without diastasis on weight-bearing views

metadata.claim_text: Weight-bearing radiographs of the Lisfranc joint reveal diastasis that may be subtle on non-weight-bearing films.

metadata.description: Protected weight-bearing and immobilization for stable nondisplaced Lisfranc patterns.
Talocalcaneal articulation governing hindfoot alignment after calcaneus and talus injuries.
Central foot region including navicular, cuneiforms, and cuboid linking hindfoot to forefoot.
Tibiotalar and subtalar articular bone with limited vascular supply.

metadata.claim_text: Labeling midfoot pain after athletic or low-energy injury as a simple sprain without checking Lisfranc alignment.

metadata.description: Persistent midfoot collapse and pain after inadequately treated Lisfranc injury.

metadata.claim_text: Operating on every Lisfranc injury without distinguishing stable nondisplaced patterns suitable for immobilization.

metadata.description: Radiographic tarsometatarsal diastasis or instability on weight-bearing views.
